text.title: security-policy-as-code Core Rules
text.summary: Migrated testable rules for security-policy-as-code. Verify and refine before relying on for production.

rule.statement: Deploy policy engine in audit mode first. Review all audit violations and remediate existing resources before switching validationFailureAction to Enforce.
rule.rationale: Enabling enforcement before fixing existing violations blocks all pending deployments in all teams simultaneously, causing incidents and eroding trust in the security programme.

rule.statement: Every policy MUST have unit tests: `kyverno test` for Kyverno policies, `conftest verify` for OPA/Rego. Tests MUST run in CI alongside application tests.
rule.rationale: Policy author hallucinations are common — LLMs emit Kyverno YAML that uses non-existent fields or wrong API versions. Without automated tests, broken policies reach the cluster silently.

rule.statement: Version-control all policies in Git. Apply policies to the cluster via GitOps (ArgoCD, Flux) — never with kubectl apply from a local machine.
rule.rationale: Manual kubectl apply bypasses PR review, audit trail, and rollback capability. A GitOps sync loop ensures cluster state always matches the repository.
